rows.summary: OLD Item 16 broadly restricts franchisees to selling only approved cryotherapy-related services and supplies, with franchisor discretion over the System image and modifications. NEW Item 16 significantly expands restrictions: prohibits all non-iCRYO business activity at the location; bans wholesale/distribution; limits sales to one physical location; imposes mandatory membership agreement terms (including term capping, approval, and no modification without consent); introduces binding reciprocity obligations (requiring acceptance of assigned memberships and permitting reassignment of memberships based on member visit patterns); and adds new operational mandates (e.g., no unapproved loyalty programs, in-person compliance meetings at franchisee’s cost). Franchisor discretion is explicitly broadened and repeated across multiple new obligations.

oldEvidence.quote: You may offer for sale only products and services We approve. You must offer Whole Body Cryotherapy, Local Cryotherapy, and Compression Therapy under Our Marks
We may change the System or any part of the System at any time, and as changed it will remain the System.
There are no restrictions on Our right to modify the types of goods and services You will offer except that We will remain a provider of Cryotherapy services primarily.

newEvidence.quote: You may not conduct any other business or activity at the iCryo Cryotherapy Center without our written permission.
All membership agreements will be subject to our current reciprocity program. We require you to offer memberships that allow members access to any System cryotherapy center.
We reserve the right to assign memberships to you and you will be required to accept and honor these memberships.

rows.itemTitle: RESTRICTIONS ON WHAT THE FRANCHISEE MAY SELL
rows.summary: The prohibition on unauthorized products is significantly expanded and codified with greater legal specificity, including explicit enumeration of banned cannabinoids, regulatory evolution language, and an affirmative obligation to monitor changing federal/state law — transforming a static list into a dynamic, compliance-intensive restriction.
rows.statedReason: Regulatory definitions and legal classifications for these products continue to evolve at the federal and state levels, and certain products that may be lawful at one time or in one jurisdiction could become prohibited or subject to stricter regulation (e.g., changes to the federal definition of “hemp” to exclude products with certain cannabinoid contents, effective November 12, 2026).

oldEvidence.quote: You may not offer or sell any unauthorized products or services, including but not limited to, cannabis products, restrictive tobacco products, consumable/ingestible CBD products, Kratom, K2, Spice, Delta variants and/or nicotine-based products or those containing THC.
Additionally, you may not market, sell or distribute from your Business any products that have received a market denial order (MDO) from the U.S. Federal Drug Administration.

newEvidence.quote: You may not offer or sell any products or services that are subject to a Denial Order issued by the Food & Drug Administration or that are not authorized in writing by us, including, without limitation, any products containing or derived from controlled substances, cannabis (whether hemp or marijuana), or cannabinoids that are regulated as controlled substances or prohibited by applicable federal, state, or local law.

Regulatory definitions and legal classifications for these products continue to evolve at the federal and state levels, and certain products that may be lawful at one time or in one jurisdiction could become prohibited or subject to stricter regulation (e.g., changes to the federal definition of “hemp” to exclude products with certain cannabinoid contents, effective November 12, 2026).

You must comply with all current and future laws governing the sale, distribution, labeling, age restrictions, taxation, and licensing of such products and refrain from selling any product that becomes prohibited by law or by us.

rows.itemTitle: RESTRICTIONS ON WHAT THE FRANCHISEE MAY SELL
rows.summary: The franchisor expanded the definition of 'Competitive Activity' by adding 'competitive with' to clauses 1 and 2, broadened territorial scope of Ancillary Business Services from 'Protected Territory' to 'Territory', and replaced 'TAB Services' for 'services and products' in multiple places — tightening restrictions on franchisee autonomy and increasing franchisor discretion over what may be sold or offered.

oldEvidence.quote:
Ancillary Business services within or outside
your Protected Territory

The Operations Manual describes the services and products
that you will offer and sell in your TAB Business

newEvidence.quote:
Ancillary Business services anywhere within or
outside your Territory

The Operations Manual describes the TAB Services you will
offer and sell in your TAB Business

rows.itemTitle: RESTRICTIONS ON WHAT THE FRANCHISEE MAY SELL
rows.summary: OLD permitted co-branding with prior written consent and allowed test marketing; NEW eliminates co-branding entirely, removes test marketing obligation, introduces mandatory future system-wide product/service offerings, adds price-setting authority for promotions, and formalizes supply chain control with reserved right to change designated vendors — all framed as unconditional franchisee obligations.
rows.newDocument: The Dolly Llama__EFD442180__DOC112888__2023 TDLF FDD v6I clean after NY Response.pdf

oldEvidence.quote: You may not operate any co-branding system without our prior written consent, which may be withheld unless we recognize the co-branding chain as an approved co-brand
We may, on occasion, require you to test market products and/or services at your Dolly Llama Shop.
You cannot sell Authorized Products on the Internet.

newEvidence.quote: You must offer and sell only products and services which are part of The Dolly Llama system.
You must offer and sell all services and products and services that we designate as required for all Franchisees within your market area
We reserve the right in the future to designate alternate vendors from whom you will purchase any required items.

rows.itemTitle: RESTRICTIONS ON WHAT THE FRANCHISEE MAY SELL
rows.summary: The franchisor changed the defined business name from 'WOSD Business' and 'WOSD Franchised Business' to 'SDCO Business' and 'SDCO Franchised Business' throughout Item 16, indicating a rebranding that alters the contractual identity of the franchise system and triggers corresponding updates to all references to the branded business entity.

oldEvidence.quote: You may not use the Business premises for any purpose other than the operation of a WOSD Business, unless otherwise approved by us in writing.
You must maintain proper permits and licenses to operate a WOSD Franchised Business and provide products and services in your area.

newEvidence.quote: You may not use the Business premises for any purpose other than the operation of a SDCO Business, unless otherwise approved by us in writing.
You must maintain proper permits and licenses to operate a SDCO Franchised Business and provide products and services in your area.
